topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, designed to work with various AI chat models including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and Mistral.

entries.q: How can I contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: You can contribute by adding prompts at prompts.chat/prompts/new, where your submissions will sync automatically to the library.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free, interactive guide that teaches prompt engineering, covering over 25 chapters from basics to advanced techniques like chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning.

entries.q: What is the target audience for the 'Prompting for Kids' section?
entries.a: The 'Prompting for Kids' section is designed for children ages 8-14, teaching them how to communicate with AI through interactive games and puzzles.

entries.q: What are some features of self-hosting prompts.chat?
entries.a: Self-hosting allows you to deploy a private prompt library with custom branding, themes, and authentication options. A setup wizard assists with configuration.

entries.q: What integrations are available for prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat can be integrated with various tools, including a CLI for command-line access, a Claude Code Plugin for AI tools, and it can function as an MCP server.
Prompts.chat has been featured in Forbes, referenced by Harvard and Columbia, and has over 143k stars on GitHub, making it a well-regarded resource in the AI community.
